The Problem How to Fix It The Total Where to Start
Overhead Analysis FY2025 & Automation Roadmap / LA  ·  7 min read

The $8.75M you already spend.

$26.5M in revenue. $269K kept. One dollar in three goes to labor no client ever sees. This is where it goes, and six ways to recover it.

Built from the LA office's 2025 financials, full timesheet export, and a workflow bottleneck audit. Every number traces back to those three sources.
Create 2025 Financials.pdf LA office full-year P&L, departmental budgets, revenue, margin, bonus exposure
2025 Hours - Full Company.csv 112,801 timesheet records. Every hour logged by LA staff, full year
Workflow Bottleneck Audit 16-page audit by Brandon Parvini. Internal tools, process inefficiencies, 20+ tool portfolio
01 — THE PROBLEM

Revenue works. Margin doesn't.

Revenue up 18% year-over-year. Margin 85% below budget. The gap is operational, not commercial.

$26.5M
Revenue
$8.75M
Non-billable labor
$269K
Net income (1.0%)

49% gross margin vs. 56% budget. Seven points of gap, driven by producer utilization. When producers spend time on admin instead of client work, margin shrinks.

31.3%
Producer non-utilization (vs 26.4% goal)
13.2%
Editor non-utilization (vs 10% goal)
Producer group revenue-to-cost ratios range from 0.6x to 7.7x. Films & Series closed at a $1.2M net loss, while paying $227K in bonuses. The 2026 bonus plan adds a profitability gate: if the department loses money, bonuses go to zero. That structural change alone recovers $200K-$300K annually.

02 — WHAT’S ALREADY CHANGED

The foundation is already in place.

Two SaaS migrations, a new bonus structure, three Auris platforms, and a 20+ tool dev portfolio shipped in Q1 2026. These are the tools behind the six opportunities that follow. Click any row below to expand.

PlatformStatusImpact
Notion Deployed Foundation for Opportunities #1, #2, and #6. Every automation in this report run...
What It Does
Replaced Trello, Monday.com, Milanote, and Lucid. Project databases, task tracking, resource views, wikis, and documentation in one platform across every department.
Why It Matters
Foundation for Opportunities #1, #2, and #6. Every automation in this report runs through Notion.
MediaSilo In Progress Wiredrive hit its ceiling. MediaSilo shortens review cycles and protects client ...
What It Does
Wiredrive replacement. Time-coded annotations, frame-level commenting, side-by-side version comparison, watermarked sharing, Adobe integration, metadata search. Frame.io under evaluation alongside it.
Why It Matters
Wiredrive hit its ceiling. MediaSilo shortens review cycles and protects client IP. Supports Opportunity #5. Complements CaraOne.
2026 Bonus Plan Deployed Back-tested against 2025: F&S bonuses ($227K) eliminated entirely. $200K-$30...
What It Does
Dual-gate structure. Individual GP must hit 90%+ of target (below = $0). Department net income sets the multiplier (breakeven or loss = 0%). Miss either gate, no payout.
Why It Matters
Back-tested against 2025: F&S bonuses ($227K) eliminated entirely. $200K-$300K reduced exposure annually, flowing directly to net income.
Auris Markets Shipped Revenue tool. 22 users, 255 sessions in 90 days. Surfaces deal opportunities. Bu...
What It Does
Aggregates trade news, cross-references upcoming releases against Create's portfolio via embedding-based match scoring. AI chat interface, pipeline tracking, branded portfolios, Notion integration.
Why It Matters
Revenue tool. 22 users, 255 sessions in 90 days. Surfaces deal opportunities. Built in-house, $0 external cost.
Auris Weekly Intelligence Shipped 172 recipients, 58% open rate. Games and Film editions near 100%. 8x platform re...
What It Does
Automated weekly newsletters (Games, Films & Series editions). AI-written pitch analysis, matched releases, podcast intelligence, event calendars. Sent company-wide every Monday.
Why It Matters
172 recipients, 58% open rate. Games and Film editions near 100%. 8x platform reach at $0 additional cost.
Auris Storyboards In Dev $54K invested ($36K spent, ~$18K remaining). Targets 458 hours of storyboard lab...
What It Does
On-prem LLM tool generating illustrated storyboard panels from scene descriptions. Multi-stage pipeline with beat tracking and version management. Runs locally for IP security.
Why It Matters
$54K invested ($36K spent, ~$18K remaining). Targets 458 hours of storyboard labor + $35K in freelance costs ($59.5K/yr). Investment case turns on automation percentage once live.
Auris Cue Sheets In Dev 972 hours on cue sheets in 2025 ($29K), 44% of one person's work week. 72% autom...
What It Does
Ingests .prproj files and extracts music usage data. Phase 1 targets sequence breakdown: manually selecting tracks in Premiere to get durations.
Why It Matters
972 hours on cue sheets in 2025 ($29K), 44% of one person's work week. 72% automatable, saving ~$21K/yr. Phase 1 is under review for in-house development at no external cost, which would eliminate the payback concern entirely.
Internal Dev Portfolio
Built by Brandon Parvini, Design Director. No contractors. No additional hires.
ToolStatusImpact
COS (Studio Hub) Deployed Most-adopted internal tool. Every designer uses it daily. Launch point for LoadB...
What It Does
Single desktop app replacing scattered Notion pages, network drives, email, and chat. Project discovery, resource access, brand guides, artist directory, wiki, real-time scheduling. Full design department adoption, rolling into finishing and gaming.
Why It Matters
Most-adopted internal tool. Every designer uses it daily. Launch point for LoadBalance, Auto_Hours, and future tools. One hub instead of six places to check.
Auto_Hours Beta Timecard reconstruction is pure waste. Auto_Hours eliminates the guesswork. 867 ...
What It Does
Plugins tracking active files across After Effects, Cinema 4D, Premiere, Photoshop, Illustrator. Captures calendar events via Microsoft Graph API, detects Teams calls. Generates draft timecard entries for FileMaker. Replaces 15-30 min/week per artist.
Why It Matters
Timecard reconstruction is pure waste. Auto_Hours eliminates the guesswork. 867 hours/year recovered across the studio. Sharper time data improves project costing and producer visibility.
LoadBalance + CFO Reports In Dev LoadBalance gives producers real-time capacity they currently negotiate over cha...
What It Does
LoadBalance: real-time project assignment and bandwidth scoring for 50-person team, Notion-integrated. CFO Reports: automated monthly reporting from QuickBooks, Bill.com, FileMaker, generating formatted Excel and PDF.
Why It Matters
LoadBalance gives producers real-time capacity they currently negotiate over chat. Directly implements Opportunity #2. CFO Reports eliminates monthly manual assembly for finance.
Client Review Platform Beta Every client touchpoint: branded, centralized, password-protected. Going live st...
What It Does
Screening Portal (MediaSilo integration, password-gated, feedback collection), CRef Full (branded moodboards, daily use, password-protected), production website with brand microsites. Figma plugin auto-populates decks. Seven tools consolidated into three.
Why It Matters
Every client touchpoint: branded, centralized, password-protected. Going live studio-wide for LA and London. Replaces Wiredrive, saving $20K/yr.
Production Pipeline Partial Project kickoff to final render in one pipeline. AE Pipeline replaces separate p...
What It Does
One After Effects script unifies LA and London: project init, naming, versioning, handoff. Network rendering with font preflight and Monotype auto-activation. Premiere plugin for key/BPM shifting. Asset tracking and legal compliance. C4D render farm visibility.
Why It Matters
Project kickoff to final render in one pipeline. AE Pipeline replaces separate per-studio scripts. Network rendering no longer fails on missing fonts.
Dev Infrastructure Active How one person built 20+ tools. Knowledge system prevents tribal knowledge loss....
What It Does
Automated security scoring (Green/Yellow/Red tiers, Semgrep SAST). Agent Tennis co-development protocol lets domain experts co-build tools. 31-entry knowledge database auto-loaded into every dev session. Self-maintaining R&D portfolio in Notion.
Why It Matters
How one person built 20+ tools. Knowledge system prevents tribal knowledge loss. Governance gives IT automated oversight. Agent Tennis scales tool development to non-developers.
03 — WHERE THE MONEY GOES

Where the $8.75M goes.

Seven addressable cost pools totaling $5.93M of the $8.75M. The remainder sits in structural overhead (facilities, insurance, fixed admin) outside the scope of automation. Six of the seven are recoverable.

04 — SIX OPPORTUNITIES

How to recover it.

Six workflows, six builds. Click any row to expand.

01
Producer Workflow Automation
Est. annual savings
$350K – $650K/yr Medium confidence
$3.81M in producer non-billable time. The automatable portion (data entry, timesheets, status tracking, resourcing) is estimated at 40-50%. Recovery rate adjusted to reflect that Notion reduces but does not eliminate coordination overhead.
Solution
Notion fixes the foundation. Project databases replace manual data entry, centralize status tracking, and eliminate redundant updates across systems. Templates, automations, and relational views handle the repetition. Claude layers on top: auto-populating time entries from calendar data, detecting patterns across projects, generating status updates from the database.
Build It
Phase 1 (Notion): Project databases, templates, automations. Status, tasks, and resource notes in one system. Already in progress: COS (deployed, daily use across design) runs project discovery and resource access. The AE Pipeline Wrap tab automates handoff submission to Notion.
Phase 2 (Claude): Summarization, auto-drafting, smart routing via API. Teams and Slack for real-time updates. FileMaker-to-Notion middleware. Same architecture as Auris. Already in progress: Auto_Hours (in beta) generates draft timecards from application activity, calendar events, and Teams calls across 50+ artists.
02
Intelligent Resource Scheduling
Est. annual savings
$100K – $200K/yr Medium confidence
$658K in bench time, juggling, and downtime (2025, pre-Notion). Daily resource calls and Notion tracking went live in Q1 2026, meaning a portion of this is already recovered. Estimate reflects the remaining gap.
Current State
The foundation is live. Resource tracking runs in Notion. Daily morning calls coordinate assignments across producers. In 2025, none of this existed. Producers negotiated assignments over Teams and Slack with no capacity view. The $658K reflects that process. The question now: how much has the current system already recovered, and what does the next layer add.
Next Layer
Demand forecasting. Notion has the data. Claude can read the pipeline and predict resource needs before producers flag them in the morning call. The daily call becomes a confirmation step, not a discovery step.
Buy (if needed): Float or Runn. Purpose-built for agencies, ~$10K/year. Fast scheduling and visibility, but limited customization and no AI-driven forecasting.
Build (100% solution): Custom scheduling engine inside Auris. Notion as the data layer, Claude for demand prediction, Teams/Slack for notifications. Harder initial lift, but you own every edge case. Already in development: LoadBalance (Electron + Notion API) provides real-time project assignment and bandwidth scoring for the full 50-person team. Currently blocked on IT production database setup. When deployed, this directly implements the custom build option.
03
AI-Assisted Footage Breakdown
Est. annual savings
$113K – $169K/yr High confidence
$282K/year in manual footage review, logging, and selects. All before creative work begins.
Solution
CaraOne: on-prem AI media discovery with semantic search, auto-transcription, scene detection, emotion analysis, and cross-storage indexing. Runs on a 2U GPU server in Create's infrastructure. Client IP never leaves the building. Also feeds Opportunity #5 (Finishing Pipeline).
Deploy (On-Prem SaaS)
CaraOne is an on-prem AI appliance (2U GPU server, up to 20 active users per node). Based on Create's storage requirements, estimated cost is ~$30K/year. One server covers the 13 core media users in LA (editors, AEs, music, capture, control room). Post houses using CaraOne report 70-90% reduction in clip discovery time.

Investment: ~$30K/year. Against $113K-$169K in annual savings from this opportunity alone, 3.8-5.6x return.
Combined with #5: Against $181K-$278K/year in combined savings from #3 and #5, 6-9x return.

All processing stays on-prem. No client footage or scripts leave Create's infrastructure. MediaSilo (in progress) will handle review and presentation. CaraOne handles media discovery, AI search, and asset indexing.
04
Music Search Automation
Est. annual savings
$108K – $151K/yr High confidence
$216K in search time with Disco, SourceAudio, and Chordal (emerging, commercial music) in place. Per our head of music: no existing platform has cracked the core problem. Teams fall back to Apple Music, Spotify, and local servers because they're searching by feel, not keywords. AIMS (AI music search API) solves this and is already integrated with Disco.
Solution
The catalogs are there (Disco, SourceAudio, Chordal), but keyword search fails when the brief is "something that feels like this reference track." AIMS is an AI music search API already integrated with Disco. Prompt search (describe what you need in plain language), similarity search (upload a reference track), and video-to-music matching. No custom build required.
Activate (SaaS)
Activate AIMS in Disco. AIMS is already available as an integration inside Disco, which the music team uses daily. Prompt search, similarity search, lyrics search, and video-to-music matching. Activation timeline: days, not months. Cost: API subscription (pricing requires a quote from AIMS). No custom build, no internal dev time. If SourceAudio coverage is needed, AIMS also offers a standalone platform and API that can index additional catalogs.
05
Finishing Pipeline Automation
Est. annual savings
$68K – $109K/yr High confidence
$273K across prep, conform, mastering, and delivery. Repetitive work is the target.
Solution
Automated transcoding, naming convention enforcement, deliverables generation from client specs, AI-powered QC flagging. DaVinci Resolve supports scripting. CaraOne handles asset discovery and indexing. MediaSilo handles review and delivery. Connect them.
Build + Integrate
Build: DaVinci Resolve automation scripts for transcoding and conform. CaraOne for asset indexing and discovery, MediaSilo for review and delivery. Aspera for client handoff, LucidLink for remote editor access. Already built: Shotmap (production-ready v6.0) handles asset tracking and legal compliance. Auto-classifies assets, detects vendor sources, generates PDF/Excel reports for client handoff. Claude parses client deliverable specs and generates transcode and naming configs. The finishing team defines the rules. Claude codifies them.
06
Admin Overhead Reduction
Est. annual savings
$40K – $85K/yr High confidence
$212K in general admin. Meetings aren't the target. The recoverable portion: manual tracking, tool-switching, duplicated entry.
Current State
Notion already reduces a share of this. Consolidating from Trello, Monday.com, Milanote, and Lucid into one system cuts context-switching and redundant entry. What remains is email, internal communication, HR/compliance, and operational work. Some is irreducible. Some raises a harder question: whether the admin time reflects real overhead or insufficient billable work for certain roles. Already in progress: Auto_Hours (in beta) eliminates the 15-30 minutes per week each artist spends reconstructing timecards. COS (deployed) consolidates project discovery, brand guides, and resource access into one hub.
What the Data Shows
The 2025 data shows wide variation in non-billable ratios within the same role. Most editors run 30-38% NB. A few run 60-95%. Most designers run 5-20% NB. Notion and daily resource calls now make this visible in real time. The lever here is less automation and more visibility: ensuring headcount matches workload.
05 — THE BOTTOM LINE

All six opportunities combined.

Projected Impact

~$30K/yr

$0
Incremental build cost
Three of the six builds are already underway. New external spend: CaraOne ($30K/yr). Internal labor (one staff member, ~40-50% of time redirected to development): ~$60-75K in opportunity cost. Total effective investment: ~$90-105K/yr. Net return: 10-17x against the addressable savings.
06 — PEOPLE IMPACT

Who gets freed up. Who gets right-sized.

LA only. Two categories below. "Freed up" means automation removes process work so the same people take on more billable projects. No headcount change. "Right-size" means the data supports absorbing natural attrition rather than backfilling, because automation increases each group's throughput.

~89
LA headcount (avg monthly)
$14.8M
Total labor cost
$9.0M
Gross non-billable cost (loaded)
Department by Department

Impact assessment

Group HC Total Cost NB % What Changes Outcome
Producing Org ~25 $5.1M 90%+ Workflow automation (#1) + COS + Auto_Hours increase capacity per group Right-size
Editorial 27 $3.7M 37.5% CaraOne (#3) reduces footage breakdown. More creative time per editor Freed up
Designers 42 $3.5M 25.2% Already efficient. COS + LoadBalance reduce wait time between assignments Freed up
Music 3 $439K 94.3% Search automation (#4) + Auris Cue Sheets (deployed) target core workflow Evaluate
Finishing 7 $477K 40.0% Pipeline automation (#5) reduces NB incrementally No change
Assistant Editors 10 $531K 44.3% CaraOne (#3) reduces manual logging and selects work Freed up
Overhead 24 $2.0M 96.5% Accounting, Admin, HR, IT, Control Room, Capture, PA, Copywriting, BD Monitor IT
Producers cover cost at 5.1x overall, but group-level variance is wide (0.6x to 7.7x). The bonus plan gates individual performance. Workflow automation increases group throughput. Together, a path to right-sizing through attrition. 2-4 fewer support positions saves $200K-$400K annually. Editorial, design, and AEs gain capacity, not headcount cuts. Music (3 people, 94% NB) needs evaluation after Q2.

Staffing Plan
Producers: reduce 2-4 support roles through attrition
Absorb departures rather than backfilling as automation increases group throughput. Protect the leads. $200K-$400K in annual savings.
Editorial + Design + AEs: no reduction. Increase utilization
Higher revenue per head, not fewer heads. Monitor utilization quarterly via Notion.
Music: evaluate after Q2 automation rollout
If tools convert time to billable creative work, the group holds. If not, restructure.
Finishing: no change
Incremental improvement. 40% NB is within range.
IT: monitor post-Notion
Simplified stack should reduce support load. Evaluate in Q3.
07 — PRIORITIZATION

Where to start.

Impact vs. effort. Start top-left.

Annual Savings →
Implementation Effort →

Music Search: $108K-$151K

Lower Impact / High Effort

Finishing Pipeline: $68K-$109K

Recommended sequence

Month 1 CaraOne (#3) + activate AIMS in Disco (#4). Highest ROI, lowest effort. Both can start immediately.
Month 2-3 Resource scheduling (#2). Already in development. Blocked on IT database setup. Unblock IT, ship LoadBalance.
Month 3-6 Producer workflow automation (#1). Largest savings, longest build. Foundation already in place. One division first, then expand.
Month 6-9 Finishing pipeline (#5) + admin reduction (#6). Lower impact, but builds on the infrastructure from months 1-6. Admin reduction is already in progress via Notion and COS adoption.
